text:
The story begins when I accidentally burn my toast. My mom sends me to bed. Instead, I sneak downstairs. I find my dad polishing antique furniture.

"What are you doing?" I ask.

"Honing my craft," Dad says.

"But why?" I insisted. "It looks ugly."

He laughs. "We collect it."

"That's crazy," I said.

"Why?" He hands me a piece.

"It reminds me of grandma." I imagined her serene smile.

He puts it in my hand. I gaze.

"I think it's cool."

He smiles proud. Maybe he has grandchildren. We sit.

He shares funny old stories. It's peaceful.

I drift. That's when I remember dreams. About a sea creature.

Next morning, Dad sees it. A tear rolls.

"That reminds me!" he chuckles.

We walk to the pier.

"I saw you."

"They just leave." My dad strokes.

"A sea turtle?"

"Eats worms."

He laughs. There's adventure. I'll
